description: Mechanical verification registry for documented issues. Source of truth for bug existence proof.

issues.title: export_memories omits resolved field from episodes
issues.description: export_memories SELECT for episodes does not include the resolved column. Exported JSONL episodes lose their completion status. import_memories also does not restore resolved, so round-trip export/import resets all episodes to resolved=0.

issues.title: remote index uses SELECT instead of cursor.lastrowid
issues.description: do_store() retrieves the last inserted memory ID via SELECT ... ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1 instead of cursor.lastrowid. Under concurrent inserts for the same agent_id, this could return the wrong ID for remote vector index registration.

check.pattern: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id.*ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1

issues.title: calibrate_threshold modifies global VECTOR_MIN_SIMILARITY without agent isolation
issues.description: do_calibrate_threshold() writes to the global VECTOR_MIN_SIMILARITY variable. In a multi-agent environment, calibrating for agent A changes the threshold for all subsequent recall calls including agent B. Fixed by _agent_thresholds dict + _get_vector_threshold() — recall uses per-agent value, global is only updated when agent_id is empty.

issues.title: episode recall results lack msg_id, invisible in messages output
issues.description: _search_episodes_fts() and _search_vector() episode results do not include a msg_id field. In do_recall(), the condition 'if r.get("msg_id")' is False for episodes, so msg["id"] is never set. Episode results in the messages array have no identifier, making them unaddressable by callers.

issues.title: Profile injection in recall queries missing user_id filter — cross-user profile leakage
issues.description: _recall_cascade() and _recall_rrf() query profiles with only agent_id filter (WHERE agent_id = ?), ignoring the user_id column. If per-user profiles exist, any user's recall will inject the most recently updated profile regardless of owner. do_get_profile() correctly uses AND user_id = '' but the recall paths do not.

check.pattern: profiles WHERE agent_id = \? AND user_id = '' ORDER BY

issues.title: Recall echo chamber — recall_count boost creates positive feedback loop for frequently recalled memories
issues.description: _compute_confidence() uses recall_count to BOOST scores (log(1+count)*RECALL_BOOST), making frequently recalled memories more likely to return. Added RECENT_RECALL_PENALTY: memories recalled within 5 minutes get 0.7x score penalty to break the echo chamber.

issues.title: calibrate_threshold crashes with KeyError(float) — float missing from _VALIDATORS
issues.description: auto_tool() dispatches parameters via _VALIDATORS[typ]. The float type was never registered, so calibrate_threshold (which uses z_factor: float) raised KeyError(float) on every call. str(KeyError(float)) returns "<class 'float'>", surfaced as {"error": "<class 'float'>"}. Fixed by adding validate_float to validation.py and registering it in _VALIDATORS.

issues.title: No callback idempotency check — duplicate callback responses silently ignored as not_found
issues.description: handle_callback_respond removes callback from pending_callbacks on first call. Second call gets not_found status silently. Added ProcessedCallbacks ring buffer (200 entries) for explicit idempotency tracking.
